cases.description: ISERROR is TRUE for a #DIV/0! error, one of ANY error type it catches (per support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/is-functions-0f2d7971-6019-40a0-a171-f2d869135665)
ISERROR is also TRUE for #N/A specifically (unlike ISERR, which excludes #N/A) -- contrast with ISERR_na and ISNA_na in the sibling test files
ISERROR is TRUE for a #VALUE! error (text coerced into arithmetic)
